phrases.intent_en: Can I walk through the city gate arch?
phrases.text_local: Darf man durch den Torbogen gehen?
phrases.cultural_note: In German-speaking countries, historic city gates often allow pedestrian passage through the archway, but cyclists and vehicles must yield.

phrases.intent_en: Is the tower exterior free to view?
phrases.text_local: Kann man den Turm von außen kostenlos sehen?
phrases.cultural_note: Historic towers in Freiburg often have exterior views accessible without entry fees, but interior access may require tickets.

phrases.intent_en: Where is the medieval wall section?
phrases.text_local: Wo findet man Reste der Stadtmauer?
phrases.cultural_note: Medieval wall sections in Freiburg are often integrated into the urban fabric; signage points to accessible sections.

likely_response.translation_en: Behind the archway are wall remains.

responses.english: Behind the archway are wall remains.

phrases.intent_en: Can I take photos without blocking traffic?
phrases.text_local: Darf man Fotos machen ohne den Verkehr zu behindern?
phrases.cultural_note: German traffic rules emphasize yielding to pedestrians and cyclists, especially in historic districts with narrow passages.

phrases.intent_en: Where does the tram stop near the gate?
phrases.text_local: Wo hält die Straßenbahn beim Tor?
phrases.cultural_note: Tram stops are typically well-signposted in German cities, and pedestrians may need to cross busy intersections near historic gates.

likely_response.local: Direkt vor dem Tor an der Kreuzung.
likely_response.translation_en: Right in front of the gate at the intersection.

responses.english: Right in front of the gate at the intersection.
responses.french: Direkt vor dem Tor an der Kreuzung.
